---
title: "Alibi Detect — A Rundown for AiSmartOps"
---

## 1. What it is, in one paragraph

Alibi Detect is a Python library from Seldon focused on three things: drift detection, outlier detection, and adversarial-input detection. It is the most algorithmically sophisticated of the open-source drift libraries — it ships kernel-based (MMD, LSDD), classifier-based, and context-aware drift detectors that work natively on images, text, time series, and tabular data, with both TensorFlow and PyTorch backends. Latest release is 0.13.0 (Dec 2025). Where [Evidently](evidently_aismartops_rundown.qmd) is broad-but-tabular, Alibi Detect is narrow-but-image-capable — and that is exactly the gap radiology AI needs filled.

## 2. The license situation — read this before anything else

This is the headline caveat the [OSS landscape chapter](../landscapes/OSS_Landscape_BuildingBlock_DeepResearch.qmd) flagged, and it’s been confirmed:

```text
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pre-22 Jan 2024: Apache 2.0 (versions ≤ 0.11.5)                   │
│ Post-22 Jan 2024: BSL 1.1 (versions ≥ 0.12.0, including 0.13)     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

BSL 1.1 means production use requires a commercial license from Seldon, with one carve-out that matters to you: the Additional Use Grant explicitly permits non-profit educational institutions to use it for production — provided you do not commercialize the platform or “incorporate the Licensed Work into any models, products or services sold, licensed, marketed, or offered for sale to third-parties.” After 4 years, each released version reverts to Apache 2.0 (the “Change License” / “Change Date” mechanism).

For your AiSmartOps project, this creates a concrete legal fork:

```text
 Ramathibodi-internal use only        Plan to license/sell to other
 (hospital deployment within          hospitals or external parties
 Mahidol University)                  (the OBJ-3 sandbox ecosystem)
 │                                    │
 ▼                                    ▼
 Likely allowed under                 Likely BREAKS the Additional
 BSL Additional Use Grant.            Use Grant. Either pay Seldon,
 Still: have university               stay on the last Apache 2.0
 legal counsel sign off.              version (0.11.5), or migrate
                                      to a clean alternative.
```

Practical recommendations: (a) pin to the last Apache 2.0 version (0.11.5, Jan 2024) for the platform’s redistributable core — it’s stable and contains all the algorithms you actually need; (b) use newer BSL versions only for internal Ramathibodi research code; (c) get a written legal opinion from Mahidol legal before relying on the Additional Use Grant in any deployment that touches the Medical AI Consortium (since consortium members are third parties).

## 3. The three detector families

This is the mental model. Same library, three problem types:

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                     │
│ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐     │
│ │ DRIFT            │ │ OUTLIER          │ │ ADVERSARIAL      │     │
│ │ DETECTION        │ │ DETECTION        │ │ DETECTION        │     │
│ │                  │ │                  │ │                  │     │
│ │ "Has the input   │ │ "Is THIS image   │ │ "Has someone     │     │
│ │ distribution     │ │ from a           │ │ crafted this     │     │
│ │ shifted from     │ │ different        │ │ input to fool    │     │
│ │ the reference    │ │ distribution     │ │ the model?"      │     │
│ │ at population    │ │ at the single    │ │                  │     │
│ │ level?"          │ │ sample level?"   │ │                  │     │
│ │                  │ │                  │ │                  │     │
│ │ Population       │ │ Per-sample       │ │ Per-sample       │     │
│ │ level            │ │ level            │ │ level            │     │
│ └──────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘     │
│                                                                     │
│ Use for PMS          Use as runtime       Use as runtime            │
│ monitoring           input gate           input gate                │
│ dashboard alerts     (reject OOD)         (reject attack)           │
│                                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

This three-way split is operationally distinct from Evidently’s metric/test/dashboard split. Evidently’s dimension is how you deploy (one-shot vs. CI vs. continuous). Alibi Detect’s dimension is what kind of question you’re asking. They are complementary, not redundant.

## 4. The detector catalog

```text
DRIFT (the headline strength)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 Statistical (univariate, per-feature):
 • KSDrift             Kolmogorov-Smirnov
 • ChiSquareDrift      categorical
 • CVMDrift            Cramér-von Mises
 • FETDrift            Fisher exact (binary)

 Multivariate / kernel (the IMAGE workhorses):
 • MMDDrift            Maximum Mean Discrepancy
 • LSDDDrift           Least-Squares Density Difference
 • LearnedKernelDrift  Trainable deep kernel — best for images

 Classifier-based:
 • ClassifierDrift     Trains a domain classifier; high AUC ⇒ drift
 • SpotTheDiffDrift    Interpretable variant: tells you *which*
                       features moved

 Specialized:
 • ContextMMDDrift     Conditional drift given covariates (e.g.,
                       drift in CT *holding* age & scanner fixed)
 • ChunkKSDrift        Streaming
 • MMDDriftOnline,
   LSDDDriftOnline     Sequential / online detectors with
                       controlled false-alarm rates

OUTLIER
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 • OutlierVAE          Variational autoencoder reconstruction
 • OutlierAEGMM        Autoencoder + GMM
 • Mahalanobis         Classic
 • IForest, LOF        Tabular
 • LLR                 Likelihood-ratio for images
 • PixelCNN-based      Generative-model OOD

ADVERSARIAL
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 • AdversarialAE       Autoencoder-based detector
 • ModelDistillation   Distillation residual flags evasion
```

## 5. The killer feature for radiology — `preprocess_drift`

This is the single most important architectural pattern Alibi Detect gives you, and it’s why it earns a place in the stack despite the license headache. You don’t run drift on raw 512×512×16 pixel arrays — that would be statistically meaningless. Instead, you run drift on the hidden activations of a frozen encoder:

```text
 Raw DICOM pixels      Frozen encoder        Drift detector
 ───────────────────   ─────────────────     ─────────────────
 ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐
 │              │      │ ResNet-50    │      │              │
 │ CT slice     │ ───► │ pretrained   │ ───► │ MMDDrift     │
 │ 512×512×1    │      │ on ImageNet  │      │ on features  │
 │              │      │ or RadImageNet│     │              │
 └──────────────┘      │ (or DINOv2,  │      │ p < 0.05     │
                       │ MedSAM enc.) │      │ ⇒ drift!     │
                       └──────────────┘      └──────────────┘
                           FROZEN
                         (no training)
```

In Alibi Detect this is one line:

```python
from alibi_detect.cd import MMDDrift
from alibi_detect.cd.pytorch import HiddenOutput, preprocess_drift
from functools import partial

# Frozen pretrained encoder — RadImageNet, DINOv2, or your own.
encoder = HiddenOutput(model=resnet50_radimagenet, layer=-2)
preprocess_fn = partial(preprocess_drift, model=encoder, batch_size=32)

cd = MMDDrift(
    x_ref=ref_images,  # baseline cohort
    backend="pytorch",
    p_val=0.05,
    preprocess_fn=preprocess_fn,
)

result = cd.predict(x=this_weeks_images)
# result['data']['is_drift'] → 0 or 1
# result['data']['p_val'] → significance
# result['data']['distance'] → MMD value
```

This is the radiology drift contract Evidently cannot give you. It works because pretrained image encoders compress imaging variability (scanner, protocol, dose) into a low-dimensional manifold where MMD is statistically powerful and computationally tractable.

## 6. How Alibi Detect slots into the AiSmartOps stack

Alibi Detect is not a backbone — it’s a targeted complement to Evidently. Two specific placements:

```text
 AI SMART ORCHESTRATION PLATFORM
 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┌─────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────┐
 │ DICOM in    │►│ Orchestrator │►│ Inference (Triton)  │
 │ (PACS)      │ │ (MONAI / MIG)│ │                     │
 └──────┬──────┘ └───────┬──────┘ └──────────┬──────────┘
        │                │                   │
        │ ① runtime gate │                   │ ② PMS sidecar
        ▼                │                   ▼
 ┌──────────────────┐    │          ┌──────────────────┐
 │ Alibi Detect     │    │          │ Alibi Detect     │
 │ ─ OutlierVAE     │    │          │ ─ MMDDrift on    │
 │   (reject hand   │    │          │   encoder feats  │
 │   X-rays sent to │    │          │ ─ ContextMMD     │
 │   chest model)   │    │          │   (drift after   │
 │ ─ Adversarial    │    │          │   adjusting for  │
 │   AE detector    │    │          │   age/scanner)   │
 └──────────────────┘    │          └──────────┬───────┘
        │                │                     │
        ▼                │                     ▼
 block / route           │          ┌──────────────────┐
 to fallback             │          │ Evidently        │
        │                │          │ Monitoring UI    │
        ▼                │          │ (consume drift   │
 ┌─────────────────┐     │          │ scores as just   │
 │ MLflow registry │     │          │ another metric)  │
 │ + audit log     │     │          └──────────────────┘
 └─────────────────┘
```

Placement ① — Runtime input gate (Outlier + Adversarial): a sample-level filter that runs before inference. If a hand X-ray gets routed to the chest-X-ray model, OutlierVAE rejects it. If an input has been adversarially perturbed (rare clinically, but a regulatory requirement under FUTURE-AI’s Robustness principle), AdversarialAE flags it. Latency budget: tens of milliseconds.

Placement ② — PMS drift sidecar (Drift detection): runs on batched accumulated inputs (daily/weekly), computes MMD or LearnedKernel drift against the validated reference cohort, and publishes the resulting drift score as a metric to your Evidently dashboard. This way the radiologist sees one unified surveillance UI; Alibi Detect is the engine, Evidently is the cockpit.

## 7. Where Alibi Detect specifically fits the FUTURE-AI principles

```text
FUTURE-AI principle       Alibi Detect contribution
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Robustness                → AdversarialAE; OutlierVAE for OOD; MMDDrift for
                            covariate shift detection. ★ Primary fit ★
Universality              → ContextMMDDrift to test "does this model perform
                            equivalently across hospital sites/scanners after
                            conditioning on demographics?"
Traceability              → Drift detector state can be saved/loaded with full
                            provenance — the same reference distribution your
                            PIE used is the one PMS alerts on.
Fairness                  → Subgroup drift (run MMDDrift per demographic slice).
                            But for fairness *metrics* per se, AIF360/Fairlearn
                            are the proper tools.
```

## 8. What it does not solve (the gaps)

```text
NOT in Alibi Detect                  → Pair with
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Performance metrics, ROC, F1          Evidently / scikit-learn
Performance estimation w/o GT         NannyML
Tabular EDA / data quality            Evidently / Great Expectations
Bias / fairness metrics               AIF360, Fairlearn
Explainability (SHAP, Grad-CAM)       SHAP, Captum, MONAI
Pretty production dashboards          Evidently UI, Grafana
Hospital-grade audit logging          MLflow, your platform itself
```

It’s a single-purpose specialist — drift, outlier, adversarial. Treat it that way.

## 9. Bottom line for AiSmartOps

```text
 ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Use Alibi Detect for the ONE thing it  │
 │ uniquely does well: image-aware drift  │
 │ + outlier + adversarial detection on   │
 │ raw radiology inputs (via frozen-      │
 │ encoder feature embeddings).           │
 │                                        │
 │ Pin to v0.11.5 (Apache 2.0) for the    │
 │ redistributable platform core.         │
 │                                        │
 │ Publish its outputs as metrics into    │
 │ Evidently — don't expose its UI.       │
 │                                        │
 │ Get Mahidol legal sign-off before      │
 │ relying on the BSL non-profit          │
 │ educational Use Grant for any version  │
 │ post-0.12.                             │
 └────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The mental shorthand: Evidently is your tabular monitoring backbone; [NannyML](nannyml_post_deployment_monitoring_without_ground_truth.qmd) is your no-ground-truth performance estimator; Alibi Detect is your image-pixel drift detective. Three tools, three jobs, with Evidently as the integration surface that ties them together.
